text: Patient is pregnant, confirmed by positive urine pregnancy test in clinic today (plus three positive home tests)
transcript_quote: the urine test we ran when you checked in this morning is also positive. You are definitely pregnant.

text: First pregnancy (primigravida), planned and wanted
transcript_quote: First ever. Very planned, very wanted, very terrifying.

text: Last menstrual period was end of May, then missed period
transcript_quote: My period didn't show up at the end of May.

text: Reports morning nausea lasting until about 10am, vomiting twice
transcript_quote: Queasy in the mornings — I've only actually thrown up twice, but the nausea hangs around till about ten.

text: Reports breast tenderness
transcript_quote: My chest is really sore.

text: Reports significant fatigue, fell asleep at desk
transcript_quote: I am tired like I've never been tired. I fell asleep at my desk at lunch on Tuesday.

text: Reports urinary frequency
transcript_quote: nothing weird with peeing except doing it constantly

text: Reports occasional low pulling twinges, not painful
transcript_quote: Little pulling twinges sometimes, low down. Nothing painful.

text: Denies vaginal bleeding or spotting
transcript_quote: Any bleeding or spotting? ... No bleeding, no.

text: Denies dysuria and fevers
transcript_quote: Pain when you pee, fevers? ... no

text: History of adolescent idiopathic scoliosis diagnosed at age 13, no surgery
transcript_quote: I have scoliosis — they found it when I was thirteen, adolescent idiopathic whatever-the-full-name-is. It never needed surgery

text: History of panic disorder with panic attacks, worse recently; currently not on medication, managed with breathing/routines
transcript_quote: I have an anxiety disorder — the panic kind. Actual panic attacks... No, nothing right now. I manage with breathing stuff and routines

text: Had a small panic attack in the parking lot before visit, self-managed with partner
transcript_quote: I had a small panic attack in the parking lot before this. Jess talked me down.

text: No prior surgeries
transcript_quote: No surgeries.

text: No known allergies
transcript_quote: No allergies.

text: Former smoker, quit years ago
transcript_quote: I used to smoke — quit for good years ago

text: Occasional wine use, stopped completely upon positive test
transcript_quote: Wine sometimes, but the second that test turned positive, I stopped completely.

text: Transportation barrier: car dead for a month, relies on borrowed car or 1.5-hour bus with transfer to reach clinic
transcript_quote: Our car has been dead for a month, so we borrowed her brother's... the bus from ours takes an hour and a half with a transfer.

text: Physical examination performed; uterus/abdomen findings appropriate for stage
transcript_quote: everything feels exactly the way it should for this stage

text: Fundal height evaluated
transcript_quote: checking the top of the uterus

text: Fetal heart auscultated by doppler, strong/fast heartbeat
transcript_quote: Hear that quick galloping?

text: Fetal viability ultrasound performed: single intrauterine pregnancy with strong heartbeat, measuring consistent with dates

slots.findings: single IUP, strong heartbeat, consistent with dates

transcript_quote: a single pregnancy, inside the uterus where it belongs, with a strong heartbeat, measuring consistent with your dates

text: Anemia diagnosed on CBC — red cells low
transcript_quote: Your blood count shows anemia. Your red cells are low.

text: Started ferrous sulfate 325 mg orally once daily; take with vitamin C (orange juice), avoid coffee/tea/milk; may cause constipation and dark stools

slots.instructions: take with vitamin C, avoid coffee/tea/milk

transcript_quote: ferrous sulfate — an iron tablet, 325 milligrams, once a day. Take it with orange juice or another vitamin C source, and keep it away from coffee, tea, and milk

text: Vitamin B12 injection ordered, first dose to be given today
transcript_quote: vitamin B12 — that one's an injection, and you'll get your first one today before you leave

text: Blood group typing and antibody status ordered
transcript_quote: Today we check your blood type and antibody status

text: Complete blood count / hemogram ordered
transcript_quote: a full blood count

text: Hepatitis B surface antigen test ordered
transcript_quote: hepatitis B

text: Hepatitis C antibody test ordered
transcript_quote: hepatitis C

text: HIV test ordered
transcript_quote: HIV

text: Syphilis test ordered
transcript_quote: syphilis

text: Gonorrhea test ordered
transcript_quote: gonorrhea

text: Chlamydia test ordered
transcript_quote: chlamydia

text: Rubella immunity screening ordered
transcript_quote: whether you're immune to rubella and chickenpox

text: Varicella (chickenpox) immunity screening ordered
transcript_quote: whether you're immune to rubella and chickenpox

text: Urine culture ordered
transcript_quote: a urine culture

text: Urine protein qualitative test ordered
transcript_quote: a urine check for protein

text: Urine glucose/diabetes screening ordered
transcript_quote: for sugar

text: Pap smear performed during exam
transcript_quote: a Pap smear, which we did with your exam

text: TB skin test placed on forearm; needs reading in 2-3 days
transcript_quote: a TB skin test — that's the little bubble I'll put on your forearm; someone needs to look at it in two to three days

text: Nurse visit booked in 2-3 days for TB test read (first morning slot to ease transportation)
transcript_quote: We'll book the TB check as a quick nurse visit, first slot of the morning

text: Prenatal follow-up every 4 weeks now, closer together later; appointments stacked to reduce trips
transcript_quote: visits every four weeks for now, closer together later in the pregnancy

text: Counseled to call immediately for bleeding, severe cramping, fever, or inability to keep fluids down
transcript_quote: Call immediately for bleeding, severe cramping, fever, or fluids not staying down.

text: Counseled on nausea management: small frequent snacks, crackers before rising, cold foods, ginger tea
transcript_quote: Small frequent snacks so the stomach's never empty, crackers before you even sit up in the morning, cold foods if smells are the enemy, ginger tea if you like it.

text: Counseled on back/scoliosis care: keep moving, gentle stretching, supportive shoes, report pain; will discuss labor positioning later
transcript_quote: keep moving, gentle stretching, supportive shoes, and tell me if pain shows up rather than toughing it out

text: Counseled on anxiety accommodations: quieter waiting area with chart flag, partner welcome at all visits including labor, medication options open if panic worsens
transcript_quote: You're always welcome to wait somewhere quieter — tell the front desk, they'll flag your chart, and Jess is welcome at every single visit, including labor.

text: Reassured that early-caught, treated anemia poses no danger to baby; count will be rechecked
transcript_quote: Treated, no — we've caught it early, we're correcting it, and we'll recheck your count as we go.

text: Normal pregnancy finding documented
